news.title: Three die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
news.summary: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico City on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.

news.title: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
news.summary: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

news.summary: A man is seen clambering through a window to escape black smoke in the block where some 200 people live.

news.title: A plane crashed into a tower in Beijing but China is not saying what happened
news.summary: The collision left holes on the side of the tower, but Beijing has scrubbed other visible traces of the incident.

news.title: Three-year-old rescued and taken to hospital six days after Venezuela quake
news.summary: Video footage shows rescuers cheering as the child is pulled from rubble in La Guaira state.

news.title: Canada will take part in Eurovision Song Contest 2027
news.summary: The country becomes the first new participant since Australia joined the contest in 2015.

news.title: Colorado primary election results: Melat Kiros wins – key takeaways

news.title: Algeria heads to legislative polls amid record-low turnout fear
news.summary: Post-Hirak distrust, candidate exclusions and political apathy are expected to drive low turnout.

news.title: SCOTUS upholds birthright citizenship. And, key results from Colorado's primaries
news.summary: The Supreme Court upheld birthright citizenship on constitutional grounds on the last day of its term. And, a Democratic socialist has won the Democratic primary for a U.S. House seat in Denver.

news.title: Greetings from London, where Banksy's flag man is a warning cry
news.summary: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.

news.title: World Cup's knockout rounds collide with a massive heat wave
news.summary: A major heat wave is affecting much of the eastern half of the United States this week, bringing dangerous conditions to multiple World Cup matches.

news.title: Kids with autism are prone to drowning. Florida is trying to prevent that
news.summary: Kids with autism can be 160 times more likely than other children to drown. Florida is dedicating state money to a program that prioritizes swim lessons for these kids.

news.title: A federal law bans late voter roll purges. Republicans are pushing to reinterpret it
news.summary: There's a ban on most states systematically purging voter rolls within 90 days before Election Day. Republicans are pushing courts to reinterpret that longstanding protection for eligible voters.

news.title: In Colorado primaries, a democratic socialist wins; Bennet's governor bid ends
news.summary: Democrats nominated their candidate to try to unseat a Republican House member and tapped the state's attorney general as a frontrunner for governor.

facts.text: Honey never spoils — edible honey has been found in ancient tombs.
A day on Venus is longer than its year.
Bananas are berries; strawberries are not.
